leagueBriefing: Josh Hart made a thunderous debut for John Force Racing at the Gatornationals, leading a sweep of winners that included back-to-back Funny Car champ Chad Green and a birthday victory for Richard Gadson.

games.headline: Hart dominates Gatornationals debut with John Force Racing to end winless drought
games.brief: Josh Hart couldn't have scripted a better start to his tenure with John Force Racing, securing a hometown victory at Gainesville Raceway by taking down reigning champion Doug Kalitta. After qualifying No. 1 for the first time in his career, Hart delivered a blistering 3.733-second pass at 337 mph in the final to claim his first Top Fuel Wally in nearly five years. The win marks a significant turning point for Hart, who looks like an immediate championship threat in his new seat.
bullets: Josh Hart swept the weekend at Gainesville, securing his first career No. 1 qualifier and a track speed record of 337.83 mph.
Chad Green went back-to-back in Funny Car at the Gatornationals, recording his eighth straight round win at the historic Florida track.
Matt Hartford snapped a 40-race winless streak in Pro Stock, defeating six-time world champion Greg Anderson in the final round.
Pro Stock Motorcycle champion Richard Gadson celebrated his birthday with a wire-to-wire final round victory over John Hall.
Alexis DeJoria reached her first final round in two years during her debut with a new team, ultimately finishing runner-up to Green.
games.context: The event kicked off NHRA's 75th anniversary season and the quest for the inaugural Diamond Wally trophies.
